raw_consumer_statement: I received a call from citibank regarding a past due balance. I advised them that I had not used the card. I entered the call with the dispute resolution specialist authenticated. When I spoke with her she seemed to be telling me that the only way she could dispute the transaction is if I would give her my full social security number and other personal information as well as verify whether the purchase was made near my home. When I asked about what was purchase, where, and how much she would not give me the information. I checked my credit and there was an alert that a previously inactive card had been used in XXXX for XXXX. Since the company would not process my dispute of the transaction, I disputed it on the credit reporting website. The company continues to call trying to collect payment several times a day. When I call them I just go directly into their payment menu. I don't have the card to get the number from. Now, when I answer their call I just go directly into the automated payment menu.
